name: Surprise (Scikit for Recommender Systems)

performance.notes: Cython-accelerated; moderate performance; not designed for production scale

agent_readiness.notes: Sklearn-style recommender system library for explicit ratings. from surprise import SVD, Dataset, Reader. reader = Reader(rating_scale=(1, 5)). data = Dataset.load_from_df(df[['user','item','rating']], reader). trainset = data.build_full_trainset(). algo = SVD(n_factors=100, n_epochs=20). algo.fit(trainset). pred = algo.predict(uid='user1', iid='item2'). Cross-validation: cross_validate(algo, data, cv=5). GridSearchCV: GridSearchCV(SVD, param_grid). Algorithms: SVD, SVD++, NMF, SlopeOne, KNN variants, CoClustering, BaselineOnly. Built-in: MovieLens, Jester datasets.

gotchas: Explicit ratings only — not designed for implicit feedback (use implicit library)
Not designed for production scale — pure research/prototyping tool
Sparse data format differs from pandas — requires Reader and Dataset wrappers
Development has slowed significantly — fewer updates in recent years
best_when: Learning and prototyping recommender systems with explicit ratings
Academic research comparing SVD, SVD++, NMF, KNN algorithms
When sklearn-style cross_validate and GridSearchCV is desired for RecSys
avoid_when: When active development and support is required
summary: Surprise provides sklearn-style API for collaborative filtering on explicit ratings. SVD(n_factors=100).fit(trainset). predict(uid, iid). cross_validate(SVD, data, cv=5). Algorithms: SVD, SVD++, NMF, SlopeOne, KNN variants, CoClustering. Reader + Dataset wrappers for data loading. BSD-3. Best for academic research and prototyping explicit-rating recommendations. Not for implicit feedback (use implicit) or production scale.
